questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: One of the most amazing principle in modern physics is the Heisenberg uncertainty principle. According to it. It is not possible to make a simultaneous determination of the position and the momentum of a particle with unlimited precision. The same is applicable for energy and time coordinate of a particle. $\Delta \mathrm{P} \cdot \Delta \mathrm{x}=\frac{\mathrm{h}}{2 \pi}=\Delta \mathrm{E} \cdot \Delta \mathrm{t}$. where : $\Delta \mathrm{P}=$ uncertainty in momentum; $\Delta \mathrm{x}=$ uncertainty in position; $\Delta \mathrm{t}=$ uncertainty in time, $\Delta \mathrm{E}=$ uncertainty in energy [Given $\frac{\mathrm{h}}{2 \pi}=\mathrm{t}=1.05 \times 10^{-34} \mathrm{~J} \sec \approx 6.60 \times 10^{-16} \mathrm{eV}-\mathrm{s}$.Question: The minimum velocity of a billiard ball $(\mathrm{m}=100 \mathrm{~g})$ confined to a billiard table of dimension 1 m is :-
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: Paragraph: One of the most amazing principle in modern physics is the Heisenberg uncertainty principle. According to it. It is not possible to make a simultaneous determination of the position and the momentum of a particle with unlimited precision. The same is applicable for energy and time coordinate of a particle. $\Delta \mathrm{P} \cdot \Delta \mathrm{x}=\frac{\mathrm{h}}{2 \pi}=\Delta \mathrm{E} \cdot \Delta \mathrm{t}$. where : $\Delta \mathrm{P}=$ uncertainty in momentum; $\Delta \mathrm{x}=$ uncertainty in position; $\Delta \mathrm{t}=$ uncertainty in time, $\Delta \mathrm{E}=$ uncertainty in energy [Given $\frac{\mathrm{h}}{2 \pi}=\mathrm{t}=1.05 \times 10^{-34} \mathrm{~J} \sec \approx 6.60 \times 10^{-16} \mathrm{eV}-\mathrm{s}$.Question: A particle has a rest energy of 750 MeV and life time of $4.4 \times 10^{-24} \mathrm{sec}$. The fraction of energy uncertainty to rest energy is equal to :-
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
